title: [BUG]getting cannot find name AsyncGenerator while build
author: anusha801
body:
I am using exceljs@4.1.0 ,I am on node 12.12.0, angular "@angular/compiler": "~8.2.5",
I am getting this error 
`ERROR in node_modules/exceljs/index.d.ts:1960:30 - error TS2304: Cannot find name 'AsyncGenerator'.
1960  		[Symbol.asyncIterator](): AsyncGenerator<WorksheetReader>;`

comments.author: tnitsiri
aaroncarlin
comments.body:
Same issue for me, using exceljs@4.1.1, node 12.13.0, "@angular/compiler": "8.2.11".
"ts-node": "7.0.1", and  "typescript": "3.4.5".

I have tried adding the following in tsconfig.json, with no luck: `compilerOptions: { "lib": [ "esnext", "esnext.asynciterable" ] }` 

My errors while compiling:
```
ERROR in ../node_modules/exceljs/index.d.ts:1959:30 - error TS2304: Cannot find name 'AsyncGenerator'.
1959            [Symbol.asyncIterator](): AsyncGenerator<WorksheetReader>;
```
```
../node_modules/exceljs/index.d.ts:1973:30 - error TS2304: Cannot find name 'AsyncGenerator'.
1973            [Symbol.asyncIterator](): AsyncGenerator<Row>;
```

comments.author: aaroncarlin
comments.body: Ran the updates, noticing the issue is likely that the typescript library needed some updates, but it is not compatible with Angular 8 which I am running. Will have to upgrade to Angular 9 to support `"typescript": "3.9.6"`.

comments.author: abhijithcuaspirenxt
comments.body:
i have changed the type and typescript version to this=>
 "@types/node": "^14.11.8",
"typescript": "^3.2.4"
and in the node_modules\exceljs\index.d.ts , I commented the lines showing error 
//[Symbol.asyncIterator](): AsyncGenerator<WorksheetReader>; and //[Symbol.asyncIterator](): AsyncGenerator<Row>;,
that solved my problem  , but its not the right way, I thing if the same done in that "https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs"
it may effective to every one

comments.author: HynesIP
comments.body:
> i have changed the type and typescript version to this=>
> "@types/node": "^14.11.8",
> "typescript": "^3.2.4"
> and in the node_modules\exceljs\index.d.ts , I commented the lines showing error
> //Symbol.asyncIterator: AsyncGenerator; and //Symbol.asyncIterator: AsyncGenerator;,
> that solved my problem , but its not the right way, I thing if the same done in that "https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs"
> it may effective to every one

What do those lines do. I commented them out and the NodeJS reference and it didn't effect my Angular 8 app.

comments.author: abhijithcuaspirenxt
comments.body:
try adding " "skipLibCheck": true ", inside your tsconfig.json, and undo the comment  and check whether it is working.. ,
""
{
  "compileOnSave": false,
  "compilerOptions": {
    "baseUrl": "./",
    "outDir": "./dist/out-tsc",
    "sourceMap": true,
    "declaration": false,
	  "moduleResolution": "node",
    "emitDecoratorMetadata": true,
	  "experimentalDecorators": true,
	  "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
    "target": "es5",
    "typeRoots": [
      "node_modules/@types"
    ],
    "lib": [
      "es2017",
      "dom", 
    ],
    "skipLibCheck": true,
  }
} 
""
 this is my tsconfig.json  in the angular 7, i think it will works for you...

comments.author: pcolaianni
comments.body:
Unrelated to _exceljs_, but I had a similar error and found this issue page.

I solved using:

```
"lib": [
  "es2018",
  ...
```

Instead of `"es2017"`, in _tsconfig.json_.

And packages versions:
```
"ts-node": "^10.9.1",
"typescript": "^4.8.0",
```

In an Angular 14 project.

comments.author: Subhakarpandey
comments.body:
lib": [
      "es2018",
      "dom",

    ],

I;m using Angular 17
